avalancheActivity.comment: The avalanche risk is assessed as moderate above the tree line and low below. The snow layering is still very weak, especially in the northern and eastern sectors. Here, avalanches can be triggered by even small additional loads and can reach medium size when breaking through the weak old snowpack. In some cases, there is a thin, bound layer from the last snowfall. The danger areas are mainly located in the transition from little to more snow as well as behind terrain edges and in the entrance area of gullies and bowls. Some of the avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise.
avalancheActivity.highlights: Persistent weak layer problem! Take particular care in gullies and bowls in northern and eastern exposures!

snowpackStructure.comment: The total snow depth is well below average. A thin, partly bound snowpack covers a very variable and mostly hard old snowpack. There is significantly more snow in shady gullies and bowls than on sunny slopes, which are partly bare up to 2000 metres. In the northern and eastern exposures, the snowpack is still weakened by layers with faceted crystals and floating snow (persistent weak layer problem!).

tendency.highlights:
No change in the avalanche risk is expected.
On Tuesday, the wind will be temporarily lively, even strong in exposed high areas. Apart from that, the programme will start with sunny leisure weather, with the first extensive cloud fields arriving in the afternoon. In the evening it will become cloudy from the south, with rain and snowfall becoming more frequent during the night into Wednesday and on Wednesday itself, with the snowfall level expected to be around 1300 metres from today's perspective.

weatherForecast.comment: Fog and high fog will persist in the south and east on Monday night. Snow drizzle or small snowflakes will fall in many places, especially along the peripheral mountains. Over the Upper Styrian mountains, however, the sky will clear up more and more as the night progresses. And so Monday starts very sunny and dry in the Styrian mountains. Only the high fog will remain over the foothills with an upper limit around 1300 metres above sea level. On the slopes of the peripheral mountains, it may snow or drizzle at times. The temperature at an altitude of 2000 metres will be between -7 and -2 degrees. The temperature at an altitude of 1500 metres is between -5 and 0 degrees. Winds will be weak to moderate with gusts between 20 and 40 km/h from the south-east to south-west.

avalancheActivity.comment: The avalanche risk is low. In extremely steep gullies or bowls on shady slopes, the triggering of small slab avalanches cannot be completely ruled out.
avalancheActivity.highlights: Low avalanche danger with mostly little snow.

snowpackStructure.comment: There is still significantly less snow than average. A thin, partly bound snowpack covers a very variable and mostly hard old snowpack. In shady gullies and bowls, the connection between the bound snowpack and the old snowpack is sometimes poor. In addition, angular shapes can weaken the snow base, fundament.

tendency.highlights:
No change in the avalanche risk is expected.
On Tuesday, the wind will be temporarily lively, even strong in exposed high areas. Apart from that, the programme will start out with sunny leisure weather, with the first extensive cloud fields arriving in the afternoon. In the evening it will become cloudy from the south, with rain and snowfall becoming more frequent during the night into Wednesday and on Wednesday itself, with the snowfall level expected to be around 1300 metres from today's perspective.

weatherForecast.comment: Fog and high fog will persist on Monday night. In the south and east, but especially along the peripheral mountains, snow drizzle or small snowflakes will fall in many places. Monday will often be sunny and dry in the Styrian mountains. Only the high fog will remain over the foothills with an upper limit around 1300 metres above sea level. On the slopes of the peripheral mountains, it may snow or drizzle at times. The temperature at an altitude of 2000 metres will be around -6 degrees. The temperature at an altitude of 1500 metres will be between -7 and -4 degrees. Winds will be weak to moderate with gusts between 20 and 40 km/h from south to west.

avalancheActivity.comment: The avalanche risk is low. In the northern and eastern exposures, there is still a persistent weak layer problem at altitudes above the tree line. In steep terrain, small slab avalanches can be triggered in a few places by individual winter sports enthusiasts. Avalanche prone locations are mainly at the entrance to on shady slopes and bowls as well as behind terrain edges. In general, the risk of being swept away, falling and injury outweighs the risk of burial. However, be careful in the area of terrain traps.
avalancheActivity.highlights: Low avalanche danger, but persistent weak layer problems in the northern and eastern exposures!

snowpackStructure.comment: There is still significantly less snow than average. A thin, partly bound snowpack covers a very variable and mostly hard old snowpack. There is significantly more snow in shady gullies and bowls than on sunny slopes, some of which are partly bare up to 2000 metres. In the northern and eastern exposures, the snowpack is still weakened by layers with faceted crystals and floating snow (persistent weak layer problem!).

tendency.highlights:
No change in the avalanche risk is expected.
On Tuesday, the wind will be temporarily lively, even strong in exposed high areas. Apart from that, the programme will start with sunny leisure weather, with the first extensive cloud fields arriving in the afternoon. In the evening it will become cloudy from the south, with rain and snowfall becoming more frequent during the night into Wednesday and on Wednesday itself, with the snowfall level expected to be around 1300 metres from today's perspective.

weatherForecast.comment: During the night to Sunday, medium-high clouds spread across the whole of Styria, but the peaks remained unaffected by them. In contrast, the clouds in the south-east shroud many peaks of the Randgebirge in fog. On Sunday, a low pressure system over Hungary sends dense clouds over the Styrian mountains. While the peaks in western Upper Styria will remain largely clear, Hochschwab, Wechsel and the peaks of the entire Randgebirge are likely to be repeatedly shrouded in fog. Visibility will be correspondingly limited here. Occasionally, a few snowflakes will also fall. The temperature at an altitude of 2000 metres will be around -5 degrees. The temperature at an altitude of 1500 metres will be around -4 degrees. The wind will be light to moderate with gusts of around 20 km/h.

ALBINA.generalHeadline: Persistent weak layer problem! Avalanche prone locations, especially in the northern and eastern sectors!
